sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Game objects (rows >= 9):")
for node in seg['nodes']:
    b = node['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if min(rows) >= 9:
        print(f"  id={node['id']}, color={node['color']}, pixels={node['pixels']}, bbox=[{min(rows)},{min(cols)}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}]")

# Check block area
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nBlock area (rows 33-44, cols 26-38):")
for i, line in enumerate(ascii_lines[33:44]):
    print(f"  row {33+i}: {line[26:38]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The block is still all white. SPACE with the rotated agent didn't change the block. Interesting! So the rotation affects the agent's shape but SPACE still stamps the same pattern? Or maybe SPACE with a rotated agent doesn't work because the agent is not aligned with the block?
Wait, the agent moved to the left when I pressed LEFT. Looking at the ascii, the agent is now at a different position. The agent's bbox changed from [25,26]-[31,37] to [23,16]-[35,28]. So LEFT both rotated AND moved the agent!
The agent is now at a diagonal position, shifted to the left. The block is still at [34,27]-[43,36]. The agent is no longer directly above the block, so SPACE doesn't affect the block.
So the controls are:
- LEFT/RIGHT: rotate and move the agent
- UP/DOWN: cycle through colors
- SPACE: stamp the agent's color onto the block below (only when agent is aligned with block)
- MOUSE: select colors from toolbar
Now I need to figure out how to get the agent back to its original position. Let me try RIGHT to rotate back.
Actually wait, let me reconsider. The agent moved from [25,26]-[31,37] to [23,16]-[35,28]. That's a significant shift. The agent is now rotated and positioned differently.
Let me try RIGHT to rotate back and see if the agent returns to its original position.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try RIGHT to rotate the agent back
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
